revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Isonokamifutsumitama-jinja]][[ja:石上布都魂神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石上布都魂神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine | native_name = 石上布都魂神社 | image = Isonokamifutsumitama jinja 01.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.851874|133.969715}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine'''|石上布都魂神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bizen Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Supershrine|lt=Shikinai Supershrine|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11387322|ltq=Q11387322|qid=Q11387322|1=List of Shikinaisha in Bizen Province}} ** Series ordinal: 6 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041198|ltq=Q135041198|qid=Q135041198|1=Munakatano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Fuse Shrine|lt=Fuse Shrine|qid=Q135041199|1=Fuse Shrine (Q135041199)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q415372|ltq=Q415372|qid=Q415372|1=Akaiwa}} * {{ill|Q1053688|ltq=Q1053688|qid=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}} * {{ill|Akasaka district|lt=Akasaka district|ja|赤坂郡|zh|赤坂郡|qid=Q11635439|1=Akasaka district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183069 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いそのかみふつみたまじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1260005002799 == Commons category (P373) == * Isonokamifutsumitama-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine former site|lt=Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine former site|qid=Q135197886|1=Isonokamifutsumitama Shrine former site}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岡山県赤磐市石上字風呂谷1448', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Isonokamifutsumitama-jinja}} [[JA:石上布都魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11584766}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine |Image = [[Image:Isonokamifutsumitama jinja 01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Akaiwa City|Q415372|qid=Q415372|1=Akaiwa}} Isonokami aza Furodani 1448 |Coordinates = {{Two-tier wiki coordinates degrees minutes seconds|34|51|6.3|N|133|58|13|E|display=title,inline}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |Founded = Unknown (Age of the Gods) |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
{{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|qid=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|qid=Q2959|1=October 20}}
(currently held on the Sunday closest to this date) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Map = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Isonokami
Futsumitama Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|51|6.3|N|133|58|13|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230|coord={{coord|34|51|6.3|N|133|58|13|E}}}} }} [[Image:Isonokamifutsumitama jinja 03.jpg|240px|thumb|Main sanctuary (Okunoin)]] '''Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine''' (いそのかみふつみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Akaiwa City|Q415372|qid=Q415372|1=Akaiwa}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|qid=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === At present, one deity is enshrined: * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}, it was believed to enshrine {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|qid=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}}, the sword said to be used by {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} when he slew the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the “Shrine Register” of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), following the mythological account, it is written as the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Totsuka no Tsurugi|1=UNKNOWN}}. In a source titled “Isonokami Furujingu Ryakushō,” included in *Yamato Shiryo*, published in the third year of {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}, it states: {{ill|lt=Futsu-no-Mitama|Q11059754|qid=Q11059754|lt=Furu no Sukune|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} shares the same ancestry as {{ill|lt=Kasuga no Kami|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga no Omi|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}, being a seventh-generation descendant of {{ill|lt=Amatarashihiko Kunioshihito no Mikoto|Q11443593|qid=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}; among the three sons of {{ill|lt=Yonemochitsuki Ōomochi no Mikoto|Q11604425|qid=Q11604425|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, the Ichikawa clan, in the reign of the thirteenth {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}, by petition of the deity of {{ill|lt=Isonokami Shrine|Q708859|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Furujin|1=Isonokami Jingū}}, replaced the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe no Muraji|1=Mononobe clan}} in administering the sacred treasures of the shrine and assisting in the duties of the shrine administration, and thus were granted the clan name {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe no Omi|1=Mononobe clan}}. In the reign of the seventeenth {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, an imperial command was given to the {{ill|lt=Ichikawa clan|Q11480612|qid=Q11480612|lt=Ichikawa no Omi|1=Ichikawa no Omi}} to transfer the {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Hahakiri|Q10940643|qid=Q10940643|lt=Ama-no-Hahakiri sword|1=Ame-no-Habakiri}}, which was enshrined at the Kibi Shrine, to the {{ill|lt=Isonokami Shrine|Q708859|qid=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Furujingu|1=Isonokami Jingū}}, where it was stored and additionally enshrined, and so on. === History === It is said that the enshrinement of the aforementioned {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Totsuka no Tsurugi|1=UNKNOWN}} marks the founding of this shrine. This sword is said to have been transferred during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Shrine|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} in Yamato Province, a fact also recorded in that shrine’s traditions. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}*, it is listed as a minor shrine, and in the register of the {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|qid=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}} {{ill|lt=Sōsha|Q1107129|qid=Q1107129|1=Sōja shrine}}, it is recorded as Futsumitama Shrine of Senior Second Rank, second among 128 shrines. In {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1669|Q7602|qid=Q7602|1=1669}}), {{ill|lt=Ikeda Mitsumasa|Q3148401|qid=Q3148401|1=Ikeda Mitsumasa}}, lord of the {{ill|lt=Okayama Domain|Q903497|qid=Q903497|1=Okayama Domain}}, restored a small shrine that had stood on the mountaintop. His successor {{ill|lt=Ikeda Tsunamasa|Q3148404|qid=Q3148404|lt=Tsunamasa|1=Ikeda Tsunamasa}} donated 20 koku of shrine land in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1674|Q7620|qid=Q7620|1=1674}}), and in {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1710|Q6861|qid=Q6861|1=1710}}) constructed the shrine buildings and kagura hall. Thereafter, it continued to receive the reverence of successive lords of the {{ill|lt=Okayama Domain|Q903497|qid=Q903497|1=Okayama Domain}}. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. It is believed that the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} at this time. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}), a great fire destroyed the shrine buildings on the mountaintop, and in {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1915|Q2084|qid=Q2084|1=1915}}) it was rebuilt at its present mid-slope location. On {{ill|lt=January 10|Q2243|qid=Q2243|1=January 10}}, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}}), the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Home Affairs (Japan)|Q1142265|qid=Q1142265|1=Home Ministry}} recognized that, based on its origins and history, it qualified to become a prefectural shrine, but on February 2 of that year the shrine ranking system was abolished. The mountaintop main sanctuary (Okunoin) was designated an Akaiwa City historic site on February 18, {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 59 ({{ill|lt=1984|Q2432|qid=Q2432|1=1984}}) (at the time of designation, the former Yoshii Town). === Major festivals === * {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} – Prayer Festival * {{ill|lt=June 11 (lunar calendar)|Q839755|qid=Q839755|lt=11th day of the 6th lunar month|1=June 11 (Lunisolar)}} – Summer Festival * {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|qid=Q2959|1=October 20}} and {{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|qid=Q2960|lt=21st|1=October 21}} – Grand Annual Festival (currently held on the Sunday closest to October 20) * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}} – New Harvest Thanksgiving Festival === Local information === ; Address * {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Akaiwa City|Q415372|qid=Q415372|1=Akaiwa}} Isonokami aza Furodani 1448 ; Access * About 20 minutes by taxi from {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Station|Q11647060|qid=Q11647060|1=Kanagawa Station (Okayama)}} ({{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tsuyama Line|Q7850362|qid=Q7850362|1=Tsuyama Line}}) === References === * Shinichi Yakushiji, *Ancient Kibi Seen Through Rituals*, Kibito Publishing, 1999 * *Brief History of Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine*, on-site distributed material === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === {{Commons|Category:Isonokamifutsumitama-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko Shrine|Q500763|qid=Q500763|1=Kibitsuhiko Shrine}} – the other Ichinomiya of Bizen Province === External links === * [https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/17559/ Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine page on the Okayama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines website] * [https://www.okayama-kanko.jp/spot/11233 Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine page on the Okayama Tourism Web operated by the Okayama Prefectural Tourism Federation] {{Shinto footer}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isonokami Futsumitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akaiwa City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Bizen Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Bizen Province]] [[Category:赤磐市の建築物]]
